description: Credit-ECOA mirror. The scoring/decision engine's rendered disposition for an application. Notarize-only: AGLedger notarizes what the scoring engine rendered (the FICO/VantageScore/custom model plus the lender's policy engine); the engine scores. disposition is approve/decline/refer: approve and decline can be fully automated; refer routes to a human underwriter. The bureau (CRA) is an upstream data input, not the renderer — FCRA §615(a) requires the eventual notice to state the CRA did not make the decision. reasonCodes are the candidate principal-reason codes the model emitted; the adverse-action notice carries the ones that actually drove this decision. The scoring disposition here is a placeholder for your own scoring and policy engine: wire your real model the same way the sanctions screen wires the external screening engine, so the load-bearing value on this leg is the notice it feeds.

disposition.description: approve/decline can auto-finalize; refer routes to a human underwriter.

scoreModel.description: The model that produced the score, e.g. 'FICO 9', 'VantageScore 4.0', 'keystone-ml-v3'.

score.description: The numeric score used (FCRA §615 requires this be disclosed when a score is used).

reasonCodes.description: Candidate key-factor/reason codes the model emitted (FCRA up-to-4, +1 for inquiries).

craName.description: The credit reporting agency that supplied the report/score (FCRA §615(a) — the upstream data source, not the decision-maker).
